course_description:
This course introduces students to the concepts of foundational knowledge related to professional practice frameworks. This will involve developing knowledge about the healthcare system and the frameworks and discourse that underpin delivery of safe, high quality healthcare locally and globally. There will be a focus on learning about ethical practice and legal frameworks that guide practice.

Frameworks for practice provides students with the foundational knowledge essential to commence safe professional practice. The course addresses the ethical considerations relevant to practice and explores the legal frameworks, highlights the ethical issues related to practice and examines the legal frameworks that guide professional practice. Students will also explore local and global healthcare systems and policies that support the delivery of safe, high quality care delivery, as well as the discourses and frameworks that shape these standards.
timetable:
The timetable for this course is available on the
UQ Public Timetable
.
course_aims: The course aims to develop students’ understanding and application of frameworks that guide professional practice. Additionally, it aims to provide students with knowledge related to practice in a local and global context.

learning_outcomes.description: Identify key standards and principles that guide and underpin safe, high quality healthcare delivery.
Identify key professional, legal and ethical frameworks that underpin practice.
Work in a health professional team to apply principles of professional frameworks to professional practice.
Critically examine tensions in applying legal and ethical frameworks in professional practice and for culturally diverse groups including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander populations.

assessment_details.title: Online Quizzes

assessment_details.task_description: The aim of this assessment is for students to identify key components of professional, legal and ethical frameworks that guide safe, high quality healthcare delivery and nursing practice. This assessment consists of two (2) online quizzes, each weighted at 10%. For each quiz, students will complete multiple choice and short answer questions randomly selected from a test bank of questions. Students are to refer to the course Blackboard site Assessment tab for additional details. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Quiz responses are directly submitted to Blackboard.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 7 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
Applying for an extension
Extension requests are submitted online via my.UQ. Extension requests received in any other way will not be approved.
Extension guidelines
Extension requests should be submitted prior to the assessment deadline. Requests for extensions received after the assessment item submission due date may not be approved. Late requests must include evidence of the reasons for the late request as per the guidelines in my.UQ . The extension granted should be in proportion to the period of illness or disruption caused by the exceptional circumstances and will typically be no more than the specified maximum extension length for each assessment. A maximum of three assessment requests can be submitted for each assessment, and the third request must include an Assessment Management Plan. Students who have been ill or unable to attend class for more than 4 weeks in a semester are advised to carefully consider whether they are capable of successfully completing their courses and may need to consider applying for removal of course . Students who have a disability or a chronic medical condition (including a mental health condition) are encouraged to meet with a Disability Advisor within Student Services to request a Student Access Plan (SAP) with a clause on extension requests. Students who have an SAP can apply for a 7-day extension.
Outcome of application
Extension requests are generally processed within 2 working days.
Late submission
You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late .

assessment_details.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late .

assessment_details.title: Essay - nursing practice analysis
assessment_details.task_description: The aim of this assessment is for students to demonstrate integrated analysis of legal, ethical, and cultural safety frameworks through examination of a complex nursing practice scenario. This is a structured essay of 1500 words that consists of six sections: Section 1: Introduction identifying key legal and ethical issues in the scenario Section 2: Analysis of consent, capacity, and restrictive practices Section 3: Analysis of accountability, documentation, and open disclosure Section 4: Analysis of cultural safety and family communication Section 5: Evidence-based nursing response and recommendations Section 6: Conclusion synthesising legal and ethical tensions Students must analyse the provided scenario by applying state-specific legislation, professional codes, and ethical frameworks to propose practical nursing actions that demonstrate culturally safe, legally compliant, and ethically sound practice. A word count that is within 10% of the set length (word limit) is acceptable. A word count that is outside these 10% will be penalised through a reduction of 10% of the total mark available for the assessment. Students are to refer to the course Blackboard site Assessment tab for additional details. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Students are required to submit their work as a word document file only to the TURNITIN submission portal located on Blackboard. Alternate file types (e.g PDF) must not be submitted and will not be graded.
The anti plagiarism software TURNITIN is being used at UQ. Work submitted will be subjected to a plagiarism detection process. Copies of this work would be retained and used as source material for conducting future plagiarism checks.
Please refer to ITS help if you experience difficulties in submitting your assignments in Blackboard or using TURNITIN as instructed on the companion website.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 7 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
Applying for an extension
Extension requests are submitted online via my.UQ. Extension requests received in any other way will not be approved.
Extension guidelines
Extension requests should be submitted prior to the assessment deadline. Requests for extensions received after the assessment item submission due date may not be approved. Late requests must include evidence of the reasons for the late request as per the guidelines in my.UQ . The extension granted should be in proportion to the period of illness or disruption caused by the exceptional circumstances and will typically be no more than the specified maximum extension length for each assessment. A maximum of three assessment requests can be submitted for each assessment, and the third request must include an Assessment Management Plan. Students who have been ill or unable to attend class for more than 4 weeks in a semester are advised to carefully consider whether they are capable of successfully completing their courses and may need to consider applying for removal of course . Students who have a disability or a chronic medical condition (including a mental health condition) are encouraged to meet with a Disability Advisor within Student Services to request a Student Access Plan (SAP) with a clause on extension requests. Students who have an SAP can apply for a 7-day extension.
Outcome of application
Extension requests are generally processed within 2 working days.
Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
Late submission penalty of 10% total mark is applied each day

assessment_details.title: Paired project - legal and ethical issues in practice
assessment_details.due_date: Written summaries Monday 25/5/2026 at 1pm. Individual in-person interactive oral assessment during scheduled end of semester exam period.
assessment_details.other_conditions: Time limited. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: The aim of this assessment is for students to demonstrate deep understanding of legal OR ethical principles through preparation and application to nursing practice scenarios. This assessment consists of two parts: Part A (20%): Paired preparation of written summaries for two (2) nursing practice scenarios. Working with a partner, one student focuses on legal issues while the other focuses on ethical issues. Each student prepares summaries identifying conflicts, relevant principles, evidence-based strategies, and research process reflections (800 words total, submitted individually). Part B (25%): Individual interactive oral assessment where students receive one scenario randomly selected by the marker from the prepared summaries. Each student has six (6) minutes to write notes recalling the conflict, two relevant principles, and the first nursing action a nurse should take. Each student will then have five (5) minutes to respond to three (3) questions from the marker simulating collaboration with colleagues in clinical decision making. Students are to refer to the course Blackboard site Assessment tab for additional details. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Students are required to submit their work as a word document file only to the TURNITIN submission portal located on Blackboard. Alternate file types (e.g PDF) must not be submitted and will not be graded.
The anti plagiarism software TURNITIN is being used at UQ. Work submitted will be subjected to a plagiarism detection process. Copies of this work would be retained and used as source material for conducting future plagiarism checks.
Please refer to ITS help if you experience difficulties in submitting your assignments in Blackboard or using TURNITIN as instructed on the companion website.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 7 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
All students must present together in their pair unless exceptional circumstances emerge (see assessment policy on acceptable reasons for extension). If any student is sick and unable to present during the allocated session, they must apply for an extension via my.UQ as per UQ guidelines with the required documentation (see link above). If the extension application is approved, the student will be rescheduled to a later presentation date.
The unaffected member of the pair will present individually on the original allocated session.
Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 1 To Week 8 (23 Feb - 26 Apr)
learning_activities.topic: Lectures There will be three (3) lectures throughout the semester. These interactive sessions apply and explore the online module content in real time. Lectures will also provide activities to guide and support you with course assessment. Your lecture dates and times are found on your timetable. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 1 To Week 13 (23 Feb - 31 May)
learning_activities.topic: UQ Extend Online learning modules Module 1 (weeks 1-4): What frameworks govern nursing practice? Module 2 (weeks 5-8): What principles are found in the frameworks? Module 3 (weeks 9-12): Not everything is black and white: Applying frameworks in practice Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L04

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 2 To Week 12 (02 Mar - 24 May)
learning_activities.topic: Inquiry Based Learning Inquiry Based Learning (IBL) Workshops (TUT 1 and TUT2): There will be four (4) face-to-face and one (1) online IBL workshops throughout the semester. Refer to your timetable for relevant dates and times. Resources and details for each workshop can be found in "Learning Resources" tab > "UQ Extend course content link on the Learn.UQ Blackboard site for this course. Note: your selected workshop date and time will determine your pair allocation for assessment 3. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04
